title: Update column with or without parenthesis produce different results
poc: CREATE TABLE datepoint (mydate DATE, insiderange BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE, rangename CHAR(8));
CREATE TABLE daterange (startdate DATE, enddate DATE, name CHAR(8));
INSERT INTO daterange (startdate, enddate, name) VALUES ('2012-03-01','2012-03-31','A');
UPDATE datepoint SET (insiderange) = (SELECT TRUE FROM (SELECT startdate, enddate, name FROM daterange) AS B WHERE datepoint.mydate >= B.startdate AND datepoint.mydate <= B.enddate);
UPDATE datepoint SET insiderange = (SELECT TRUE FROM (SELECT startdate, enddate, name FROM daterange) AS B WHERE datepoint.mydate >= B.startdate AND datepoint.mydate <= B.enddate);
key_bug_features: Different results from column update with or without parentheses
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, value INT, status BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE);
UPDATE test_table SET (status) = (SELECT value > 15 FROM test_table AS T WHERE T.id = test_table.id);
